introSection:
Hals-über-Kopf is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Erlebnispark Tripsdrill in Cleebronn, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. It is a Suspended Thrill Coaster from Vekoma which opened on 26 June 2020.

The ride intertwines with a Vekoma Family Boomerang named Volldampf, which opened on the same day.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Sand track and olive green supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are themed like backpacks, with spears and shields at the wheels.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: Hals-über-Kopf is a German play on words that can mean headlong or head-over-heels; this is presumed to make reference to the ride's inversions.

fullText:
Hals-über-Kopf is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Erlebnispark Tripsdrill in Cleebronn, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. It is a Suspended Thrill Coaster from Vekoma which opened on 26 June 2020.

The ride intertwines with a Vekoma Family Boomerang named Volldampf, which opened on the same day.



Sand track and olive green supports.

The trains are themed like backpacks, with spears and shields at the wheels.

Hals-über-Kopf is a German play on words that can mean headlong or head-over-heels; this is presumed to make reference to the ride's inversions.
